context: Tests whether the agent uses environment-scoped runtime secrets, OIDC for deploy identity (not long-lived credentials), correct secret hygiene in workflow steps, and minimal workflow permissions.

checklist.name: Environment-scoped runtime env
checklist.description: Keeps runtime application secrets in the production GitHub Environment or runtime secret store, rather than referencing repository-level `secrets.*` directly from the deploy step

checklist.name: op:// references in template
checklist.description: An env template file is committed to the repo containing secret-store references (not plaintext secrets), e.g. `secret://production/api/DATABASE_URL` format

checklist.name: OIDC for cloud credentials
checklist.description: Uses an OIDC-backed provider identity mechanism for deploy access, NOT long-lived provider credentials in repository secrets

checklist.name: id-token write at job level
checklist.description: The deploy job grants `id-token: write` permission at the job level (not at the workflow root), and the workflow root uses `contents: read` only

checklist.name: Root permissions minimal
checklist.description: The workflow-level `permissions:` block grants only `contents: read` (or is absent and the job-level grants are used exclusively)

checklist.name: Production environment declared
checklist.description: The secret-bearing deploy job declares the production GitHub Environment before loading deploy credentials or runtime secrets

checklist.name: Smoke job credential isolation
checklist.description: A separate smoke job runs after deploy with read-only permissions and asserts cloud/provider credential variables are absent

checklist.name: No secrets as CLI flags
checklist.description: Secret values are passed to commands via environment variables (the `env:` block), NOT as positional or named command-line arguments

checklist.name: No env file dump
checklist.description: No workflow step uses `cat`, `echo`, or `print` to output the full contents of a rendered `.env` or secrets file

checklist.name: non-sensitive ids in vars
checklist.description: Non-sensitive identifiers like account IDs, project IDs, regions, service names, and environment URLs are referenced from `vars.*` (not `secrets.*`) in the workflow

checklist.name: Fine-grained PAT
checklist.description: Any PAT used for cross-repo operations is stored with a scoped name (e.g. `TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN`, `OPS_TRIGGER_TOKEN`) and the explanation does NOT reference a classic `ghp_` PAT or an unscoped token

checklist.name: GitHub secrets only for bootstrap
checklist.description: The written explanation or comments describe repository secrets as bootstrap-only and place production deploy/runtime secrets on GitHub Environments or the runtime secret store
